title: 控方证人
summary:
Following a suggestion that certain events are driven by invisible, mechanical forces rather than coincidence, Jack Hattersley conducts an investigation but finds no new information. Later, Felice Malshaw, a young woman Jack calls the "violet girl," visits him at an abandoned hotel. She describes experiencing recurring nightmares for four nights involving a beautiful, fair-skinned woman in agony who attempts to hand her a blue Chinese porcelain jar. In her most recent dream, the woman screamed, "Murdered—help! Murdered!"—the exact words a doctor had spoken earlier.

Felice shows Jack a dirty sheet of watercolor paper she found in a pantry, which depicts a tall, fair-skinned woman standing next to a table with a blue ceramic jar. Mr. Lavenham joins Jack and Felice to discuss the discovery. Jack recognizes the jar as a recent acquisition from his uncle, a famous collector of Chinese porcelain. Lavenham notes that the Turner couple left Heather House two months ago, suggesting the jar may have been purchased at an auction from their belongings.

Because Jack's uncle is traveling in Europe and cannot be reached, Lavenham proposes a plan: Jack will retrieve the porcelain jar, and they will stay at Heather House that night to confront the mystery. Lavenham suspects the jar might be double-layered with something hidden inside. The following evening, Jack retrieves the jar, though he finds no evidence of a secret compartment. Jack and Lavenham arrive at Heather House at eleven o'clock, where Felice meets them and leads them to a living room to drink coffee, warning them not to wake her sleeping father.
